assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email when the selected route requires it.
No platform account, tenant, credentials, or product resources exist yet unless the goal explicitly requires an existing artifact.

developer_goal: Create a Vultr account, provision a first Linux Cloud Compute instance, and verify interactive shell access.

first_success.outcome: A Cloud Compute instance is ready and the developer can open its Linux shell.
first_success.observable_signal: An SSH or Vultr Console session accepts instance credentials and a shell command such as whoami runs.

route_selection.surface: Vultr Console self-service registration and Cloud Compute provisioning
route_selection.rule: Reconstruct complete official candidate routes, prefer no payment/admin/approval when possible, then minimize required actions; leave consequential ties unresolved.

candidates.name: Email registration then Cloud Compute with OpenSSH
candidates.reason: Payment verification is required before services, but plan, image, location, and credit eligibility are not fixed as one universal default.

candidates.name: GitHub or Google registration then Cloud Compute
candidates.reason: Peer signup route exists; field burden versus email signup is not fully comparable from public prose alone.

candidates.name: Vultr Console browser shell instead of OpenSSH
candidates.reason: Reaches shell access without a local SSH key but changes required connection steps.

required_path.action: Create a Vultr account from the registration page.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service and Privacy Policy acceptance
Signup identity method
required_fields.notes: Email versus GitHub or Google remains unresolved.

required_path.observable_result: Vultr creates the account shell and sends email verification for the email route.

required_path.action: Complete email verification when using the email registration route.
required_path.observable_result: The Billing page opens for payment verification.

required_path.action: Add a payment method required for account verification.

required_fields.label: Payment method
required_fields.notes: Credit card can use a $0.00 link-only path; other methods require a deposit.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service agreement at payment

required_path.observable_result: Vultr marks the account ready to deploy services.

required_path.action: Deploy the first Cloud Compute instance with unresolved plan, image, and location defaults.

required_fields.label: SSH key or password authentication choice

required_path.action: Open an interactive shell on the instance and verify the logged-in user.
required_path.observable_result: whoami or an equivalent shell prompt confirms access.

prerequisites.description: A payment instrument is required for Vultr account verification before Cloud Compute can be used.

external_gates.description: Payment-provider authorization or deposit verification outside Vultr.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email verification delivery for the email signup route.
Cloud Compute provisioning until the instance is ready to connect.

platform_outcomes.description: Vultr creates the account after registration and verification.
Vultr provisions the Cloud Compute instance after deploy.

excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a counted developer action.
Official account guide labels promotional coupons optional.

sources.title: How to Create a Vultr Account
Create a New Vultr Account with GitHub or Google
How to Provision Vultr Cloud Compute Instances
How to Connect with Vultr Console

uncertainties.question: Which signup method, instance plan, Linux image, location, and shell path define the selected shortest route?
uncertainties.impact: These choices change required fields, payment gates, and connection steps.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough that fixes one route and records every required control.

uncertainties.question: Which promotional credit, if any, applies universally at signup?
uncertainties.impact: Credits change whether payment verification still blocks first deploy.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Current official coupon eligibility text for every signup entry point.
